problem: The Helix CLI ticket commands (get, artifacts, artifact, rerun, continue) pass raw user input directly to /api/tickets/{ticketId} endpoints which require internal IDs. Short IDs (BLD-339) and numeric ticket numbers (339) return 404. No --help handling exists in any subcommand router. No --json output flag is supported. Run timestamps render as Invalid Date. No test infrastructure exists.

files.why: Ticket subcommand router. Contains resolveTicketId() (lines 13-26) which extracts raw ticket reference from --ticket flag, HELIX_TICKET_ID env, or first positional arg and passes it directly to API calls without any ID resolution. All ticket subcommands (get, rerun, continue, artifacts, artifact, bundle) route through here. No --help/-h check before dispatch.

Implements tickets get command. printTicketDetail() (lines 25-60) calls /api/tickets/{ticketId} with raw ID. Truncates description at 500 chars (line 57). Run timestamps use new Date(run.createdAt).toLocaleString() (line 50) which produces Invalid Date for unparseable values. No --json flag support. Also exported and used by latest.ts.

Implements tickets list command. cmdTicketsList() outputs formatted text only (line 88). Omits internal id field from output despite TicketItem type having it. No --json flag support. TicketItem type (lines 5-12) shows fields available from /api/tickets endpoint: id, shortId, title, status, updatedAt, reporter.

Implements tickets latest command. Fetches /api/tickets and calls printTicketDetail() with first item's internal id (line 45). Demonstrates that list endpoint returns internal IDs that could be used for client-side resolution.
Implements tickets artifacts command. Calls /api/tickets/{ticketId}/artifacts with raw ticketId (line 18). Needs resolver adoption.
Implements tickets artifact command. Calls /api/tickets/{ticketId} and /api/tickets/{ticketId}/runs/{runId}/step-artifacts/{stepId} with raw ticketId (lines 33, 42). Needs resolver adoption.
Implements tickets rerun command. POSTs to /api/tickets/{ticketId}/rerun with raw ticketId (line 9). Needs resolver adoption.
Implements tickets continue command. POSTs to /api/tickets/{ticketId}/rerun with raw ticketId (line 24). Needs resolver adoption.
Implements tickets bundle command. Calls /api/tickets/{ticketId} with raw ticketId (line 33). Needs resolver adoption.
Implements tickets create command. Does not take a ticket reference (creates new tickets). May need --help handling but does not need resolver.

Main CLI entry point and command dispatcher. SKIP_AUTO_UPDATE set includes --help/-h (line 43) but main switch (lines 51-94) has no case for --help/-h — these fall through to default which prints Unknown command and usage. No global help interception.
Shared flag parsing: getFlag, hasFlag, getPositionalArgs, requireFlag. No help-specific utilities. hasFlag could be used to check --help/-h but is not currently used for that purpose anywhere.

HTTP client hxFetch(). All ticket API calls route through this. Builds URL from config.url + basePath + path. Handles retry, auth headers, timeout. Error messages include HTTP status (line 34) which is what users see for 404s.
HxConfig type definition (lines 12-19) includes url, apiKey, orgId, orgName. requireConfig() used by ticket commands. orgId/orgName available for error messages about current org context.

Repository resolution pattern: exact ID match -> exact name match (case-insensitive) -> partial name match. Analogous pattern could be used for ticket resolution. Fetches list from API then matches client-side.
Inspect subcommand router. No --help/-h check. inspect db query is joined from positional args (line 29) with no --query flag alternative.
Inspect db command. Takes repoNameOrId and query string. No --query flag; query comes from positional args only, causing PowerShell quoting issues with Postgres quoted identifiers.
Comments subcommand router. Has its own separate resolveTicketId() (lines 6-14) duplicating tickets/index.ts logic. Demonstrates the duplication that shared resolution should eliminate.
Project metadata. No test runner (jest/vitest/mocha) in dependencies. Scripts: build (tsc), typecheck (tsc --noEmit), prepare (npm run build). No test or lint scripts.
TypeScript config. strict: true, ES2022 target, Node16 module system, output to dist/. Tests would need to be integrated with this config.

facts: resolveTicketId() in src/tickets/index.ts (lines 13-26) extracts raw user input and passes it directly as the ticketId to API endpoint URLs without any ID-type detection or resolution
A duplicate resolveTicketId() exists in src/comments/index.ts (lines 6-14) with identical logic minus positional arg support
All 7 ticket subcommands that take a ticket reference (get, artifacts, artifact, rerun, continue, bundle, and latest indirectly) use hxFetch with /api/tickets/{rawInput} URL pattern

No subcommand router (tickets, inspect, org, token, comments) checks for --help or -h before dispatching. The main index.ts switch has no case for --help/-h despite including them in SKIP_AUTO_UPDATE set
No --json flag is checked or supported in any ticket command (list or get)
tickets list text output (list.ts line 88) omits the internal id field even though TicketItem type includes it
tickets get truncates description to 500 characters (get.ts line 57) with no option to get full text
Run timestamps in get.ts (line 50-51) use new Date(run.createdAt).toLocaleString() which produces 'Invalid Date' when input is not parseable by Date constructor
completedAt null check (get.ts line 51) correctly shows 'in progress' for null but does not validate the actual run status field
src/lib/resolve-repo.ts demonstrates the existing pattern for name-to-ID resolution: fetch list from API, then match client-side by exact ID, exact name, or partial name
tickets latest (latest.ts line 44-45) demonstrates that the /api/tickets list endpoint returns objects with internal id fields that could be used for client-side ticket resolution

inspect db (inspect/index.ts line 29) joins positional args as query string with no --query flag alternative, creating shell quoting difficulties for PowerShell with Postgres double-quoted identifiers
No test files exist in the repository. No test runner in package.json dependencies. No CI configuration files found
TypeScript typecheck (tsc --noEmit) passes cleanly on the current codebase
Package has build script (tsc) and typecheck script (tsc --noEmit) as the only quality gates
HELIX_CLI_NOTES.md referenced in ticket was not found at workspace root or in helix-cli directory
unknowns: Whether the backend API supports ticket lookup by shortId or numeric ticket number directly (e.g., /api/tickets/BLD-339 or /api/tickets?shortId=BLD-339), or if client-side resolution via the list endpoint is the required approach

Whether the /api/tickets list endpoint response includes all fields needed for reliable client-side resolution (it includes id, shortId, title, status, updatedAt, reporter — but unknown if ticketNumber is a separate field or derivable from shortId)
What timestamp format the API returns for run createdAt/completedAt fields and what specific values trigger the Invalid Date rendering
Whether the /api/tickets list endpoint supports pagination and if resolution needs to handle paginated results for orgs with many tickets
What test framework the team prefers since none is currently configured (node:test is available with Node>=18 and would add zero dependencies)
Whether the HELIX_CLI_NOTES.md field report was committed to a different branch or was never persisted
